data.title: Curve Founder Highlights Risks in Non-Isolated Lending Models
data.description:
Curve founder Michael Egorov has expressed concerns over the recent attack on Kelp DAO, which resulted in losses of approximately $292 million and led to bad debt formation on Aave. According to Foresight News, Egorov attributes these issues to the prevalent use of non-isolated lending models, which, while offering good scalability, come with higher risks. He emphasizes the importance of risk management in such models.

Egorov suggests that adopting a fully isolated model, similar to Curve Finance's market, or a hybrid model, though complex, could mitigate these risks. However, he notes that the market has yet to fully understand the advantages of these approaches. The Hub and Spoke model of Aave v4 is seen as a potential step towards a semi-isolated and safer direction.

data.title: Lido: EarnETH deposits have been suspended due to the Kelp security incident; stETH and the staking protocol are unaffected.
data.description: Lido posted on the X platform that he is aware of the Kelp DAO security incident. Due to the current exposure of the earnETH product to rsETH, new deposits to earnETH have been suspended as a precaution. He is currently assessing the situation with relevant partners and will disclose more information as it develops. Lido added that stETH and wstETH were not affected by the rsETH incident, and the security issue does not involve the Lido staking protocol itself.

data.title: Curve founder: KelpDAO attack causes Aave bad debts, exposing risks of non-segregated lending.
data.description: Curve founder Michael Egorov stated that the attack on KelpDAO resulted in approximately $292 million in losses and bad debts on Aave, reflecting the potential risks of the current "non-segregated lending" model. He pointed out that while this model has good scalability, it carries high risk and requires strengthened risk management. He also mentioned that fully segregated or hybrid models may be viable alternatives, and believes that the "hub and spoke" architecture proposed by Aave v4 may drive lending models towards a more secure direction.

data.title: ASTEROID's market capitalization surpasses $150 million, with a daily increase of over 550%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, ASTEROID's market capitalization briefly surged past $150 million, currently trading at $146 million, representing a daily increase of over 550%. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: KelpDAO Suffers Major Exploit Resulting in Significant Losses
data.description:
KelpDAO experienced a significant security breach on April 19, resulting in the unauthorized creation of 116,500 rsETH tokens. According to BlockBeats, the attack led to losses estimated at approximately $293 million.

The majority of the illicitly minted rsETH was deposited into Aave as collateral to borrow ETH, while a smaller portion was directly sold for ETH. The hacker managed to acquire a total of 106,466 ETH through these actions.

data.title: Aave Freezes rsETH Markets Following KelpDAO Bridge Exploit
data.description: Aave has temporarily frozen its rsETH markets in response to a significant exploit involving the KelpDAO bridge, which resulted in an estimated $177 million to $200 million in bad debt within Aave's wETH pool. According to NS3.AI, Omer Goldberg from Chaos Labs reported that the attacker managed to borrow 83,427 WETH and wstETH using unbacked rsETH across both Ethereum and Arbitrum networks. Following the news of the exploit, AAVE's price experienced an intraday decline of approximately 10%. Despite the incident, Aave confirmed that its contracts were not compromised and that withdrawals remain operational.

data.title: Lido Suspends EarnETH Deposits Following Kelp DAO Attack
data.description: Lido has announced the suspension of further deposits into earnETH due to exposure to rsETH following the Kelp DAO attack. According to Foresight News, the Lido Earn team is actively assessing the situation with relevant partners. Lido has assured users that stETH and wstETH remain unaffected by the rsETH incident, and the staking protocol continues to operate normally.

data.title: Ethereum's Kelp Protocol Suspends rsETH Contracts Amid Investigation
data.description: Ethereum's restaking protocol, Kelp, has confirmed suspicious cross-chain activity involving rsETH. According to Odaily, the protocol has paused rsETH contracts on the mainnet and several Layer 2 networks. An investigation into the incident has been initiated, with Kelp collaborating with LayerZero, Unichain, auditing firms, and security experts to conduct a root cause analysis. Further details will be released following the investigation.

data.title: Kelp Suspends rsETH Contracts Amid Suspicious Cross-Chain Activity
data.description: Kelp has temporarily halted rsETH contracts on the Ethereum mainnet and several Layer 2 networks following the detection of suspicious cross-chain activity. According to NS3.AI, the company has initiated an investigation into the potential attack and is collaborating with LayerZero, Unichain, audit firms, and security experts to conduct a thorough root cause analysis. Kelp plans to publish a post-mortem report once the investigation is complete.

data.title: Andre Cronje: Aave has no subsidy-based user loss mechanism and has withdrawn ETH to the fund management encapsulation contract.
data.description: Andre Cronje, co-founder of Sonic Labs and founder of Flying Tulip, stated on the X platform that his team is continuously investigating the L0/rsETH incident. Preliminary reports indicate that approximately $200 million worth of rsETH was stolen, possibly due to a private key leak or misconfiguration. These assets were subsequently deposited into Aave as collateral for ETH lending (due to insufficient rsETH liquidity). Cronje pointed out that the affected positions are technically still collateralized, but in the event of bad debts, Aave's token mechanism and security modules will act as the first line of defense to absorb the risk. However, Aave does not have a mechanism to compensate users for losses; otherwise, it could trigger a run on the platform. Currently, Aave holds approximately $7 billion worth of ETH and has about $100 million in withdrawals, so the overall impact of this incident is limited. Furthermore, prioritizing user liquidity, Flying Tulip has withdrawn all ETH from Aave to its fund management contract. This action was taken because Aave's available liquidity had fallen below its set minimum threshold.

data.title: Aave Chan Initiative Halts Frontier Staking Service Amid Risks
data.description:
Aave Chan Initiative (ACI) founder Marc Zeller announced on the governance forum the immediate termination of the Frontier staking service plan due to concerns over rsETH collateral and potential wETH shortages and bad debt risks in Aave V3 Core. According to Foresight News, all ETH staked through Frontier will be fully withdrawn and managed by Aave DAO to protect wETH depositors if necessary. Additionally, ACI has decided to forgo all potential income and planned fees from the service until the exit is completed.

Frontier was introduced by ACI in January 2024 as a staking-as-a-service product for Aave DAO.

data.title: Aave's founder terminated the Frontier staking program and transferred ETH to Aave DAO for risk management.
data.description: Marc Zeller, founder of the Aave Chan Initiative (ACI), announced on the governance forum that ACI has decided to immediately terminate its Frontier staking service due to the potential risks associated with rsETH that could lead to a shortage of wETH and bad debt issues for Aave V3 Core. All ETH staked through Frontier will be fully withdrawn and managed by the Aave DAO to protect wETH depositors if necessary. ACI also stated that it will forgo any potential revenue and fees associated with the service up to the point of termination.

data.title: An address holding 8.02 billion ASTEROIDs has a floating profit of $2.6 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, one address holds approximately 8.02 billion ASTEROIDs, has been holding them for over 580 days, and currently has an unrealized profit of approximately $2.6 million.

data.title: The TRUMP team transferred 7.59 million TRUMP tokens, worth approximately $21.81 million, to BitGo.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, the TRUMP token team has transferred 7.59 million TRUMP tokens, worth approximately $21.81 million, to BitGo Custody.

data.title: Kelp has suspended rsETH contracts on its mainnet and multiple L2 servers and launched an investigation into the attack.
data.description: The Ethereum restaking protocol Kelp has officially confirmed suspicious cross-chain activity involving rsETH. It has suspended rsETH contracts on the mainnet and multiple L2 servers and launched an investigation into the attack. Kelp is also collaborating with LayerZero, Unichain, auditing firms, and security experts to conduct a root cause analysis (RCA). An incident analysis will be released later.

data.title: RAVE briefly fell below $2.54, a 90.5% drop in 24 hours.
data.description: OKX market data shows that RaveDAO (RAVE) is currently trading at $2.54, down 90.5% in the last 24 hours.

data.title: AAVE Experiences Significant Drop Following Whale Sell-Off
data.description: AAVE's value has decreased by 15% due to substantial sell-offs by major holders. According to Foresight News, the decline follows a hacking incident involving the illegal minting of rsETH through Aave collateral, leading to the borrowing of a large amount of ETH. Several large-scale transactions contributed to the drop, including a sale by Polymarket user 'smaugvision,' who sold 20,015 AAVE tokens at $102.9 each, totaling approximately $2.06 million. Another transaction involved the address starting with 0xFC5, which sold 20,000 AAVE tokens at $102.8 each, amounting to roughly $2.05 million. Additionally, the address 0xA2E sold 19,665 AAVE tokens at $99.2 each, totaling about $1.95 million.

data.title: DeFi Protocol Fluid Suspends Markets Amid KelpDAO rsETH Incident
data.description:
On April 19, DeFi protocol Fluid announced the suspension of markets potentially affected by the KelpDAO rsETH incident. According to BlockBeats, Fluid clarified that it has no substantial exposure to rsETH on Layer 2 and noted that no new lending activities using rsETH as collateral have occurred following the cross-chain bridge vulnerability.

Fluid assured users that their funds remain secure and no action is required. Other Fluid markets continue to operate safely and normally. The team is actively monitoring the situation and will provide updates as necessary.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. Musk agrees to make ASTEROID the SpaceX mascot; 2. Kelp DAO's rsETH bridging protocol is suspected of being hacked, resulting in a loss of approximately $292 million; 3. Aave is suspected of incurring bad debts due to the rsETH attack, the specific amount of which is currently unknown; 4. The market capitalization of the Ethereum blockchain's Meme coin ASTEROID surpasses $130 million, with a 24-hour increase of over 400%; 5. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister: The date for the next round of negotiations with the US cannot be determined before an agreement is reached; 6. Iranian media: Iran states that it will go all out if it returns to war; 7. Institutional sources respond to DeepSeek's first external funding round: The news is very likely true, but it's currently impossible to invest; 8. Japan approves FIEA amendments, classifying digital assets as financial instruments and prohibiting insider trading; 9. ZachXBT: It is not recommended to short highly controlled tokens controlled by a small number of people.

data.title: Euler Labs Temporarily Suspends EUL Cross-Chain Bridge Amid Investigation
data.description: Euler Labs has announced a temporary suspension of the EUL LayerZero OFT cross-chain bridge as a precautionary measure. According to Foresight News, this decision comes amid an ongoing investigation into the rsETH incident. Euler Labs emphasized that EUL has no exposure to the rsETH event and will provide updates once cross-chain functionality is restored.
